NUMEROUS TREES DOWN.

STRAIGHTLINE WIND EVENT WITH ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS OF 60 TO 80 MPH. TWO MAIN DAMAGE AREAS, ONE AROUND BEAVERTON AND ANOTHER NORTH OF LAKE LANCER IN THE SUGAR SPRINGS AREA. AROUND BEAVERTON, THE WIND DAMAGE BEGAN APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF TOWN AND CONTINUED SPORADICALLY TO APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES NORTHEAST OF BEAVERTON. THE MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE WAS IN A 1/2 MILE AREA WITHIN DOWNTOWN BEAVERTON. IN THE SUGAR SPRINGS AREA, DAMAGE INVOLVED SEVERAL TREES DOWN AND SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO HOMES.

PHOTOS RECEIVED OF A WATERSPOUT ON LAKE HURON. EYEWITNESS DESCRIBED ROTATION AND FUNNEL CLOUD DEVLOPMENT OCCURRED INITIALLY OVER LAND...BUT TOUCH DOWN DID NOT OCCUR UNTIL THE PARENT SHOWER MOVED OVER THE LAKE. WATERSPOUT LASTED FOR AT LEAST SEVERAL MINUTES. TIME IS APPROXIMATE.

EF-0 CONFIRMED JUST SOUTH OF EXIT 270 ALONG INTERSTATE 75 IN EXTREME SOUTHERN OSTEGO COUNTY. TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST PAST MILE MARKER 268 AND TRAVELED FOR JUST OVER ONE QUARTER MILE. NUMEROUS EVERGREEN AND POPLAR TREES WERE DOWNED. MOST TREES WERE BLOWN COMPLETELY DOWN WHILE A FEW WERE SNAPPED. ESTIMATED PEAK WIND SPEED 65 TO 75 MPH. PATH LENGTH 0.3 MILES. PATH WIDTH 150 YARDS.
